response:
There once was a programmer named Dave,
Who laughed at warnings to behave.
Merged a glitch that wiped his whole drive,
All his late-night builds didn’t survive,
Now he backs every line up to save.

This limerick follows the classic AABBA rhyme scheme, leans into relatable programmer chaos (ignoring pre-merge warnings, lost work from bad code, the universal lesson of backing up files) and wraps up with a light, lesson-driven punchline that fits the whimsical tone of the form.
